hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/5 interactions through electrostatic repulsion, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve SSTR2 selectivity. A focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: To target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.
Introduce mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target interactions while maintaining SSTR2 binding.
